title: senml: inconsistent error types for unsupported CBOR features
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 87, 200-215, 363-377

The codebase has inconsistent error handling for unsupported CBOR features:

1. enc_head (line 87): Returns CborError::NotImplemented for values > 65535
2. dec_head (lines 200-215): Returns CborError::InvalidInput for info 26/27 (32/64-bit lengths)
3. skip_one_depth (lines 363-377): Returns CborError::InvalidInput for info >= 26

These represent the same situation (unsupported CBOR feature) but use different error types. This makes it difficult for callers to distinguish between:
- Malformed CBOR (truly invalid input)
- Valid CBOR using features this implementation doesn't support

Impact: Confusing error handling; callers cannot distinguish correctable errors from implementation limitations.

Fix: Use CborError::NotImplemented consistently for valid-but-unsupported CBOR constructs:
- dec_head lines 215: change to NotImplemented
- skip_one_depth line 377: change to NotImplemented
